sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Clicking on the sky blue square changed the board. The board changed from having 7 nodes to 9 nodes. Two white (W) 1-pixel nodes appeared (nodes 1 and 7), and the black border nodes changed from 64 pixels to 63 pixels each. This suggests that clicking on the sky blue square placed white dots somewhere on the border. Let me check where these white pixels are.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Find where the white pixels are
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'W':
        print(f"White node {n['id']}: boundary={n['boundary']}, pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Also check the black border nodes
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'B' and n['pixels'] == 63:
        print(f"Black border node {n['id']}: boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL RESULT: python
sections.content:
White node 1: boundary=[[0, 63]], pixels=1
White node 7: boundary=[[63, 0]], pixels=1
Black border node 0: boundary=[[0, 0], [0, 62]]
Black border node 8: boundary=[[63, 1], [63, 63]]

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
So clicking on the left sky blue square placed white dots at:
- (0, 63) - top-right corner
- (63, 0) - bottom-left corner
This seems to be marking the corners diagonally opposite to where the square is. The left square is on the left side, so it marks the right-top and left-bottom corners. Let me try clicking on the right sky blue square to see what happens.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try clicking on the right sky blue square (node 6, center at row 51, col 41)
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 51, 'col': 41}])
print("Result:", result)
print("Board changed:", result.get('board_changed'))
print("Gameplay changed:", result.get('gameplay_changed'))

# Check white pixels now
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
print("\nAll nodes:")
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'W':
        print(f"  White node {n['id']}: boundary={n['boundary']}, pixels={n['pixels']}")
    elif n['color'] == 'B' and n['pixels'] < 100:
        print(f"  Black border node {n['id']}: boundary={n['boundary']}, pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
